description: Fix CSRF token handling so that the CSRF cookie is properly set during login/signup, the middleware properly validates tokens, and the client-side provider can read and attach them correctly.
steps: Step 1: Fix lib/auth/api-handlers.ts - Change response.headers.set('Set-Cookie', ...) to response.headers.append('Set-Cookie', ...) in both POST_login (around line 133) and POST_signup (around line 310). The .set() method OVERWRITES any existing Set-Cookie headers (including the session cookie from setSessionCookie()), while .append() adds an additional header. This is the root cause of the CSRF cookie never being set.

Step 2: Create a proper middleware.ts file at the project root (/projects/sandbox/nucrm-bigplan-by-vm-enterprise-v2/middleware.ts) that exports a middleware function and config. This should import and call the proxy function from proxy.ts. The proxy.ts file already has the correct CSRF validation logic but was never wired as Next.js middleware. The middleware.ts should: export { proxy as middleware } from './proxy'; export { config } from './proxy';

Step 3: In proxy.ts, fix the CSRF validation error message to be more user-friendly. Change 'CSRF token missing or invalid' to 'CSRF token missing or invalid. Please refresh the page and try again.' (matching the message in lib/auth/middleware.ts requireCsrf function). Also add /api/tenant/onboarding/ to the needsCsrfValidation exemptions in lib/auth/csrf.ts if not already there (it IS already there, so confirm).

Step 4: Verify the CsrfProvider (components/shared/csrf-provider.tsx) properly handles the case where getCsrfToken() returns null. Currently when token is null, it skips attaching the header entirely (which is correct - the request will go through without the header, and GET requests don't need it). The issue is that for POST/PUT/DELETE when cookie is missing, the middleware will block it. Add a fallback: if the CSRF cookie is not found and the method requires CSRF, fetch /api/auth/csrf-token to get a fresh token set. Create a new minimal API route at app/api/auth/csrf-token/route.ts that generates a CSRF token, sets the cookie, and returns 200.
acceptance_criteria: Login response sets BOTH the nucrm_session cookie AND the nucrm_csrf_token cookie (verified by checking both headers.append calls exist)
middleware.ts exists at project root and exports the proxy function as middleware
A /api/auth/csrf-token endpoint exists that can regenerate CSRF tokens
CsrfProvider handles missing token by fetching a fresh one before retrying the request
The build passes (npx next build completes without errors)
verification: Run: npx next build (or verify TypeScript compilation via npx tsc --noEmit) to confirm no type errors
Run: npx vitest run tests/unit/security-fixes.test.ts tests/unit/auth-modules.test.ts to confirm CSRF tests pass
findings: All CSRF-related tests pass (tests/unit/security-fixes.test.ts: 12 passed). auth-modules.test.ts is excluded in vitest config. TypeScript compiles cleanly for all modified files. Pre-existing TS errors in app/tenant/contacts/[id]/page.tsx are unrelated.
